optionA.text: Body with a distinct incision just posterior to the setigers carrying the cage-forming setae

optionB.text: Neurosetae entirely simple, but usually distinctly cross-barred

optionA.text: Body encased in a smooth continuous mucus sheath

optionB.text: Body with individual papillae covered with mucus and impregnated with debris

optionA.text: Branchial membrane long, tonguelike, sometimes doubled

optionB.text: Branchial membrane short, rounded or triangular

optionA.text: Branchial membrane club-shaped, with branchial filaments attached distally on all sides

optionB.text: Branchial membrane flattened, with branchial filaments attached on one side only
At least some neurosetae acicular or falcigerous

optionA.text: Body anteriorly inflated with tapering posterior end

optionB.text: Body short, flattened and nearly disc-shaped

optionA.text: Cephalic cage poorly developed or absent
Neurosetae distinctly thicker than notosetae, four pairs of branchiae

optionB.text: Neurosetae only slightly thicker than notosetae; numerous pairs of branchiae

optionA.text: Body covered by a thick mucus sheath

optionB.text: Body not covered by a mucus sheath, often sand-incrusted
